title: CatBoost ordered-CTR interaction sidecar
summary: The table has high-cardinality Driver plus Race/Race_Year interactions.
rationale: The table has high-cardinality Driver plus Race/Race_Year interactions. CatBoost is designed to build ordered target-statistic features for categorical variables and combinations while reducing target leakage compared with manual global encodings.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoostClassifier with native categorical columns Driver, Race, Compound, Race_Year and engineered categorical crosses like Race_Year_Compound, Driver_Race, Compound_Stint, and binned TyreLife/RaceProgress. Generate OOF predictions under Race_Year-grouped folds and blend/rank-average with the current AutoGluon or GBDT predictions.
expected_effect: Could add ranking signal from sparse strategy identities that frequency encoding and generic AutoGluon handling may underuse, especially driver-race and compound-stint habits.
risk: CatBoost CTRs must be fit inside each fold; random row folds can leak race identity and inflate local CV.
